text.title: Roadmap Design Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: B2B SaaS and solo product Now-Next-Later roadmap examples, and common roadmap antipatterns with fixes.

example.note: Now (Q1): onboarding improvements (reduce time to value), API v2 launch (enterprise requirement), performance optimization (load times). Each tied to objective: "Improve activation rate to 40%".
Next (Q2): team collaboration features (confidence: High), Slack integration (confidence: Medium), advanced permissions (confidence: Medium). Depends on: API v2 stable.
Later: mobile app (pending user research validation), AI-powered insights (exploring, no commitment). Explicitly not doing: custom themes, white-labelling — not on enterprise ICP roadmap this year.
Now: core feature polish toward MLP, payment integration, basic analytics.
Next: email automation, template library, referral program.
Later: mobile app, team features, marketplace. Not doing: multi-language support — insufficient international user demand to justify.

antipattern.description: Feature list with specific dates for every item.
antipattern.reason: Dates imply commitment. When any date slips, stakeholder trust erodes. Use confidence levels instead of dates for everything beyond "Now".

antipattern.description: No strategic connection — initiatives not linked to objectives.
antipattern.reason: Orphaned initiatives accumulate and eventually dominate the roadmap without anyone being able to justify why. Every initiative must have an objective ID.
If items never graduate from Later to Next, stakeholders stop believing the roadmap is real. Review Later quarterly and either promote, archive, or move to not-doing with a reason.

antipattern.description: Same roadmap shared with engineering, exec, and customers.
antipattern.reason: Each audience needs a different abstraction level. Engineering needs initiative detail; exec needs theme-level strategy; customers need benefit language with no internal jargon.
